subcommands.name: A
subcommands.description: Append archive to the end of another archive

name: --check-device
options.description: Check device numbers when creating incremental archives

name: --level
options.description: Set dump level for created listed-incremental archive

name: --no-check-device
options.description: Do not check device numbers when creating incremental archives

name: --ignore-failed-read
options.description: Do not exit with nonzero on unreadable files

name: --restrict
options.description: Disable the use of some potentially harmful options

name: --remove-files
options.description: Remove files from disk after adding them to the archive

name: W
-W
--verify
options.description: Verify the archive after writing it

name: --atime-preserve
options.description: Preserve access times on dumped files

suggestions.name: replace
suggestions.description: Restore the times after reading

suggestions.name: system
suggestions.description: Not setting the times in the first place

name: --group
options.description: Force NAME as group for added files

args.name: NAME[:GID]
args.description: If GID is not supplied, NAME can be either a user name or numeric GID

name: --group-map
options.description: Read group translation map from FILE

args.name: FILE
args.description: Each non-empty line in FILE defines translation for a single group

name: --mode
options.description: Force symbolic mode CHANGES for added files

name: --mtime
options.description: Set mtime for added files

args.name: DATE-OR-FILE
args.description: Either a date/time in almost arbitrary format, or the name of an existing file

name: --owner
options.description: Force NAME as owner for added files

args.name: NAME[:GID]
args.description: If UID is not supplied, NAME can be either a user name or numeric UID

name: --owner-map
options.description: Read owner translation map from FILE

args.name: FILE
args.description: Each non-empty line in FILE defines translation for a single UID

name: --sort
options.description: When creating an archive, sort directory entries according to ORDER

name: --add-file
options.description: Add FILE to the archive

name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude files matching PATTERN

args.name: PATTERN
args.description: A glob-style wildcard pattern

name: --exclude-backups
options.description: Exclude backup and lock files

name: --exclude-caches
options.description: Exclude contents of directories containing file CACHEDIR.TAG, except for the tag file itself

name: --exclude-caches-all
options.description: Exclude directories containing file CACHEDIR.TAG and the file itself

name: --exclude-caches-under
options.description: Exclude everything under directories containing CACHEDIR.TAG

name: --exclude-ignore
options.description: Read exclusion patterns from FILE in directory before dumping

name: --exclude-ignore-recursive
options.description: Same  as --exclude-ignore, except that patterns from FILE affect both the directory and all its subdirectories

name: --exclude-tag
options.description: Exclude contents of directories containing FILE, except for FILE itself

name: --exclude-tag-all
options.description: Exclude directories containing FILE

name: --exclude-tag-under
options.description: Exclude everything under directories containing FILE

name: --exclude-vcs
options.description: Exclude version control system directories

name: --exclude-vcs-ignores
options.description: Exclude files that match patterns read from VCS-specific ignore files

name: h
-h
--dereference
options.description: Follow symlinks; archive and dump the files they point to

name: --hard-dereference
options.description: Follow hard links; archive and dump the files they refer to

name: N
-N
--newer
--after-date
options.description: Only store files newer than DATE

args.name: DATE
args.description: If DATE starts with / or . it is taken to be a file name

name: --one-file-system
options.description: Stay in local file system when creating archive

name: P
-P
--absolute-names
options.description: Don't strip leading slashes from file names

name: --anchored
options.description: Patterns match file name start

name: --ignore-case
options.description: Ignore case

name: --no-anchored
options.description: Patterns match after any /

name: --no-ignore-case
options.description: Case sensitive matching

name: --no-wildcards
options.description: Verbatim string matching

name: --no-wildcards-match-slash
options.description: Wildcards do not match /

name: --wildcards
options.description: Use wildcards

name: --wildcards-match-slash
options.description: Wildcards match /

name: --clamp-mtime
options.description: Only set time when the file is more recent than what was given with --mtime

name: l
-l
--check-links
options.description: Print a message if not all links are dumped

name: a
-a
--auto-compress
options.description: Use archive suffix to determine the compression program

name: I
-I
--use-compress-program
options.description: Filter data through COMMAND

name: j
-j
--bzip2
options.description: Filter the archive through bzip2

name: J
-J
--xz
options.description: Filter the archive through xz

name: --lzip
options.description: Filter the archive through lzip

name: --lzma
options.description: Filter the archive through lzma

name: --lzop
options.description: Filter the archive through lzop

name: --no-auto-compress
options.description: Do not use archive suffix to determine the compression program

name: z
-z
--gzip
--gunzip
--ungzip
options.description: Filter the archive through gzip

name: Z
-Z
--compress
--uncompress
options.description: Filter the archive through compress

name: --zstd
options.description: Filter the archive through zstd

name: --transform
--xform
options.description: Use sed replace EXPRESSION to transform file names

name: --checkpoint
options.description: Display progress messages every Nth record

name: --checkpoint-action
options.description: Run ACTION on each checkpoint

name: --full-time
options.description: Print file time to its full resolution

name: --utc
options.description: Print file modification times in UTC

name: b
-b
--blocking-factor
options.description: Set record size to BLOCKSx512 bytes

name: --record-size
options.description: Set record size

args.name: NUMBER
args.description: The number of bytes per record

name: H
-H
--format
options.description: Create archive of the given format

suggestions.name: gnu
suggestions.description: GNU tar 1.13.x format

suggestions.name: oldgnu
suggestions.description: GNU format as per tar <= 1.12

suggestions.name: pax
suggestions.description: POSIX 1003.1-2001 (pax) format

suggestions.name: ustar
suggestions.description: POSIX 1003.1-1988 (ustar) format

suggestions.name: v7
suggestions.description: Old V7 tar format

name: --old-archive
--portability
options.description: Same as --format=v7

name: --pax-option
options.description: Control pax keywords when creating PAX archives

name: --posix
options.description: Same as --format=posix

name: V
-V
--label
options.description: Create archive with volume name TEXT

name: --warning
options.description: Enable or disable warning messages identified by KEYWORD

suggestions.name: all
suggestions.description: Enable all warning messages

suggestions.name: none
suggestions.description: Disable all warning messages

suggestions.name: filename-with-nuls
suggestions.description: "%s: file name read contains nul character"

suggestions.name: no-filename-with-nuls
suggestions.description: No "%s: file name read contains nul character"

suggestions.name: alone-zero-block
suggestions.description: "A lone zero block at %s"

suggestions.name: no-alone-zero-block
suggestions.description: No "A lone zero block at %s"

suggestions.name: cachedir
suggestions.description: "%s: contains a cache directory tag %s; %s"

suggestions.name: no-cachedir
suggestions.description: No "%s: contains a cache directory tag %s; %s"

suggestions.name: file-shrank
suggestions.description: "%s: File shrank by %s bytes; padding with zeros"

suggestions.name: no-file-shrank
suggestions.description: No "%s: File shrank by %s bytes; padding with zeros"

suggestions.name: xdev
suggestions.description: "%s: file is on a different filesystem; not dumped"

suggestions.name: no-xdev
suggestions.description: No "%s: file is on a different filesystem; not dumped"

suggestions.name: file-ignored
suggestions.description: "%s: Unknown file type; file ignored", "%s: socket ignored", "%s: door ignored"

suggestions.name: no-file-ignored
suggestions.description: No "%s: Unknown file type; file ignored", "%s: socket ignored", "%s: door ignored"

suggestions.name: file-unchanged
suggestions.description: "%s: file is unchanged; not dumped"

suggestions.name: no-file-unchanged
suggestions.description: No "%s: file is unchanged; not dumped"

suggestions.name: ignore-archive
suggestions.description: "%s: file is the archive; not dumped"

suggestions.name: no-ignore-archive
suggestions.description: No "%s: file is the archive; not dumped"

suggestions.name: file-removed
suggestions.description: "%s: File removed before we read it"

suggestions.name: no-file-removed
suggestions.description: No "%s: File removed before we read it"

suggestions.name: file-changed
suggestions.description: "%s: file changed as we read it"

suggestions.name: no-file-changed
suggestions.description: No "%s: file changed as we read it"

suggestions.name: failed-read
suggestions.description: Enables warnings about unreadable files or directories

suggestions.name: no-failed-read
suggestions.description: Suppresses warnings about unreadable files or directories

name: o
-o
options.description: Same as --old-archive

name: f
-f
--file
options.description: Use archive file or device ARCHIVE

name: --force-local
options.description: Archive file is local even if it has a colon

-F
--info-script
--new-volume-script
options.description: Run  COMMAND  at the end of each tape

-L
--tape-length
options.description: Change tape after writing Nx1024 bytes

-M
--multi-volume
options.description: Create/list/extract multi-volume archive

options.description: Use  COMMAND instead of rmt when accessing remote archives

name: --rsh-command
options.description: Use  COMMAND instead of rsh when accessing remote archives

name: --volno-file
options.description: Tar will keep track of which volume of a multi-volume archive it is working in FILE

options.description: Filter the archive through bzip2

options.description: Filter the archive through lzip

name: --transform
--xform
options.description: Use sed replace EXPRESSION to transform file names

name: --checkpoint
options.description: Display progress messages every Nth record

name: --checkpoint-action
options.description: Run ACTION on each checkpoint

name: --full-time
options.description: Print file time to its full resolution

options.description: Print file modification times in UTC

name: K
-K
--starting-file
options.description: Begin at the given member in the archive

name: --show-omitted-dirs
options.description: List each directory that does not match search criteria

name: k
-k
--keep-old-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files when extracting

name: --keep-newer-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files that are newer than their archive copies

name: --keep-directory-symlink
options.description: Don't replace existing symlinks to directories when extracting

name: --no-overwrite-dir
options.description: Preserve metadata of existing directories

name: --one-top-level
options.description: Extract all files into DIR

name: --overwrite
options.description: Overwrite existing files when extracting

name: --overwrite-dir
options.description: Overwrite metadata of existing directories when extracting

name: --recursive-unlink
options.description: Recursively remove all files in the directory prior to extracting it

name: --skip-old-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files when extracting, silently skip over them

name: U
-U
--unlink-first
options.description: Remove each file prior to extracting over it

name: O
-O
--to-stdout
options.description: Extract files to standard output

name: --to-command
options.description: Pipe extracted files to COMMAND

name: --delay-directory-restore
options.description: Delay setting modification times and permissions of extracted directories until the end of extraction

name: m
-m
--touch
options.description: Don't extract file modified time

name: --no-delay-directory-restore
options.description: Cancel the effect of the prior --delay-directory-restore option

name: --no-same-owner
options.description: Extract files as yourself

name: --no-same-permissions
options.description: Apply the user's umask when extracting permissions from the archive

name: p
-p
--preserve-permissions
--same-permissions
options.description: Extract information about file permissions

name: --preserve
options.description: Same as both -p and -s

name: --same-owner
options.description: Try extracting files with the same ownership as exists in the archive

name: s
-s
--preserve-order
--same-order
options.description: Sort names to extract to match archive

name: --strip-components
options.description: Strip NUMBER leading components from file names on extraction

name: --warning
options.description: Enable or disable warning messages identified by KEYWORD

suggestions.name: all
suggestions.description: Enable all warning messages

suggestions.name: none
suggestions.description: Disable all warning messages

suggestions.name: filename-with-nuls
suggestions.description: "%s: file name read contains nul character"

suggestions.name: no-filename-with-nuls
suggestions.description: No "%s: file name read contains nul character"

suggestions.description: No "A lone zero block at %s"

suggestions.description: "%s: skipping existing file"

suggestions.name: no-existing-file
suggestions.description: No "%s: skipping existing file"

suggestions.description: "Extracting contiguous files as regular files"

suggestions.description: No "Ignoring unknown extended header keyword '%s'"

suggestions.description: No "Record size = %lu blocks"

suggestions.description: No "%s: Directory is new"
